title: Test Strategy, Contracts, Properties, and Mutation · Policy Clinic: Property Tests
summary: Design a layered test suite around risks rather than file counts. Planned artifact: Add and verify the gates property tests increment in a risk-to-test coverage matrix. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use generated cases for algebraic properties, parser boundaries, and state invariants.
Test public behavior, invariants, boundaries, and failure recovery.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Build Property Tests evidence
steps.instruction: Append a gates-property-tests-18is0m: manifest entry with concrete verify plus failure, owner, or repair evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Property Tests into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
gates-property-tests-18is0m:
  verify: [replace with a deterministic check]
  failure: [replace with the rejected condition]
  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

steps.title: Defend the Property Tests model
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Property Tests applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
